# Dead Internet Tracker

> A one-page static dashboard tracking public signals related to the Dead Internet Theory.

Dead Internet Tracker compares narrow, checkable public signals rather than claiming one precise score for how much of the internet is "dead." The dashboard is static at runtime. Browser code reads local snapshot files only; there are no live browser API calls.

Canonical URL: https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/

## What This Site Measures

- AI-generated or AI-influenced content estimates from recurring public reports and curated research.
- Monthly Common Crawl samples of article-style web pages classified for AI writing signals.
- Cloudflare Radar AI bot traffic as a proxy for AI systems crawling and traversing the public web.
- Wikipedia editor activity as a proxy for sustained human contribution to open knowledge.
- Stack Overflow question volume as a proxy for public human-to-human software help.
- Imperva bot and account-attack reports as narrower security-focused automation signals.

## Important Caveats

- This dashboard does not measure the whole internet.
- Cloudflare sees a large but incomplete slice of the public web, not the whole internet.
- Imperva data is security-focused and is not directly comparable to Cloudflare.
- Common Crawl samples are article-style open-web pages, not all pages.
- Published AI-content estimates come from different studies and content sets, so levels are noisy.
- The dashboard is meant to compare signal direction, not produce a magic score.

## Core Pages

- [Dashboard](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/)
- [Project README](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/README.md)
- [Web sample methodology](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/WEB_SAMPLE_AI_CLASSIFICATION_TEMPLATE.md)

## Snapshot Data

- [Cloudflare AI bot traffic](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/cloudflare/cloudflare.json)
- [Wikipedia activity](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/wikipedia/wikipedia.json)
- [Stack Overflow activity](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/stackoverflow/stackoverflow.json)
- [Imperva traffic profile](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/imperva/imperva.json)
- [Imperva account takeover series](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/imperva/imperva_ato.json)
- [AI content meta-review](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/ai-content-meta-review/ai_content_meta_review.json)
- [Common Crawl web sample dashboard snapshot](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/web-sample/web_sample_dashboard_snapshot.json)
- [Common Crawl lite web sample summary](https://dead-internet-tracker.onrender.com/data/web-sample-lite/web_sample_lite_summary.json)

## Suggested Summary

Dead Internet Tracker is a static public-data dashboard for the Dead Internet Theory. It tracks AI-generated content estimates, AI bot traffic, Common Crawl web samples, Wikipedia editor activity, Stack Overflow question volume, and bot-abuse reports. Its main claim is cautious: no single public dataset measures the whole internet, but comparing multiple recurring signals can show whether automation is rising and public human activity is declining in measurable places.
